transferir.title: Transfer
transferir.desc: Copy playlists from one service to another easily.
transferir.heroTitle: Transfer your playlists to any platform
transferir.heroSubtitle: Move entire lists between Spotify, YouTube Music, Apple Music and Amazon Music in a few clicks — no need to rebuild anything from scratch.

steps.s1d: Link the source and target services via OAuth. Your tokens are stored encrypted.
steps.s2d: Select the list you want to move. We read every track including artist and album.
steps.s3d: We create the playlist on the destination platform with as many matched tracks as possible.

benefits.b1d: Transfers run in the background — no need to wait on the screen.
benefits.b2d: Official OAuth only. We never store your provider password.
benefits.b3d: Big playlists are paginated and batched automatically.
benefits.b4d: Use it as many times as you want while the project is in development.

sincronizar.title: Sync
sincronizar.desc: Keep your playlists always up to date.
sincronizar.heroSubtitle: Link two playlists on different services and keep them mirrored. Anything added on the source is automatically copied to the target.

steps.s1d: Link the service where you curate playlists and the one where you want the mirror.
steps.s2d: Pick source and target playlist. Both must already exist on the respective services.
steps.s3d: Every day we check the source and copy new tracks to the destination, without duplicates.

benefits.b1d: Set it once and forget — the sync runs alone in the background.
benefits.b2d: Pause, resume or delete a sync anytime.
benefits.b3d: We only add new tracks. Songs already on the target are not copied again.
benefits.b4d: Mix any combination of Spotify, YouTube Music, Apple Music and Amazon Music.

gerador.title: AI Generator
gerador.desc: Create playlists from scratch using AI with genres and moods.
gerador.heroTitle: Create the perfect playlist in seconds
gerador.heroSubtitle: Our advanced generator combines genres, moods and a free-form prompt to turn your ideas into ready-to-play playlists — all with real tracks from the major streaming services.

steps.s1d: Pick genres (Pop, Rock, Rap…), moods (Happy, Relaxed, Energetic…) or type a prompt like “Late-night Brazilian trap”.
steps.s2d: Get 20 suggestions with real cover art, remove the ones that don't fit and generate more as many times as you need.
steps.s3d: One click creates the playlist in your Spotify, YouTube Music or other linked account, ready to play.

benefits.b1d: Instead of thinking of tracks one by one, you start with 20 real suggestions and refine.
benefits.b2d: Generated tracks can be saved to any service you have a linked account with.
benefits.b3d: One click applies one of the creative ready-made names or write your own.
benefits.b4d: While the project is in beta, with no generation limit and no paywall.

partilhar.title: Share
partilhar.heroSubtitle: Send a link that opens the playlist on the recipient's favorite service — even if it's different from yours.

steps.s1d: On the dashboard, select a playlist you have already transferred.
steps.s2d: A click on Share copies the direct link from the target service.
steps.s3d: The recipient opens it in their favourite app and saves it to their library.

benefits.b1d: Official Spotify / YouTube / Apple Music links — open in the native app.
benefits.b2d: Only whoever has the link can access it; you decide what to share.
benefits.b3d: Send it as-is — nobody needs ITransferMusic to open the link.
benefits.b4d: Share button right on each playlist created by the dashboard.

hero.title: Transfer your playlists between the major streaming services
hero.subtitle: Connect Spotify, YouTube, Apple Music and Amazon Music in one place.

about.p1: For decades, our relationship with music has gone through many transformations. We remember with nostalgia the times when we collected vinyl records, cassette tapes and CDs, and each piece carried unique stories and emotions. Today, with the rise of streaming services, the way we enjoy our music has changed radically. But this new reality also brings challenges: how do you migrate that special playlist from one service to another? Or convert a collection of MP3 files into your digital library?
about.p2: That is where TransferMusic comes in. Our service makes it possible to transfer playlists between platforms like Spotify, Deezer, YouTube Music and many others, making it easy to access your favorite music without hassle. Try it out and rediscover how to enjoy your personal soundtrack, always on your own beat!

help.title: Frequently asked questions
help.subtitle: Have questions about how to transfer your music, save your playlists or sync across platforms? You're in the right place. Here's everything you need to know before you start.

faq.a1: ITransferMusic is a platform to transfer and sync playlists between the major streaming services. With official integrations for Spotify, YouTube Music, Apple Music, Amazon Music and more, our goal is to be the simplest and most reliable way to migrate your music library without rebuilding anything from scratch.
faq.a2: You connect both the source and destination accounts via official OAuth, pick the playlist you want to move, and click Start Transfer. ITransferMusic reads every track, searches for the matching entry on the destination and creates a new playlist with as many matches as possible. It runs in the background — you can close the page and come back later.
faq.a3: Yes. We exclusively use official OAuth — we never request or store your streaming account passwords. Access tokens are encrypted with Fernet (AES 128) before being persisted. You can unlink an account at any time from the settings panel, which immediately invalidates the stored tokens.
faq.q4: Do transferred playlists stay automatically in sync?
faq.a4: Not by default. Every transfer creates a static copy at the moment you clicked Start. If you want to keep both playlists mirrored over time, enable a Sync from the Sync action in the dashboard — you pick the frequency (daily or weekly), the time, and whether to only add new tracks or keep both playlists identical (mirror mode).
faq.q5: What happens if some songs can't be matched?
faq.a5: Cross-platform matching is done by textual search (track name + artist). Some tracks may not be found on the destination because of region, licensing or metadata differences (explicit versions, remasters, etc). In those cases the transfer is created as partial: you can see how many tracks matched (e.g. 42/50) and the full list is stored in the Transfer History for review.
faq.q6: Can I link multiple accounts from the same service?
faq.a6: For now only one active account per service at a time. To switch accounts — e.g., migrating from a personal profile to a professional Spotify profile — click Switch Account on the selection step: the current token is invalidated and you log in again with the new account.
faq.a7: While the project is in development, everything is completely free with unlimited transfers. We want you to try every tool before we revisit the monetization model.
faq.q8: Can I share a transferred playlist?
faq.a8: Yes. Once a transfer finishes, a Share button is available both in the dashboard and in the history. It generates a public page with the track list, covers and buttons to open the playlist in any compatible service. Only people with the link can view it, and you can edit the page name and description whenever you want.
faq.q9: What if I later delete the playlist on Spotify/YouTube?
faq.a9: ITransferMusic automatically detects when the destination playlist was removed from the provider. The card disappears from the dashboard (to avoid broken links), but the entry stays in the Transfer History with all tracks, covers, generated name and description — so you never lose what was migrated, even if the target playlist no longer exists.
